english: The baby is still too small. Another few weeks would make the difference.
irish: Ach tá sé i bhfad ró-bheag fós. Dá mbeadh cúpla seachtain eile aige fiú.

english: That'll be the case, please God.
irish: Agus beidh le cúnamh Dé.

english: I'm not even 30 weeks gone. The baby won't stand a chance.
irish: Ach má rugtar anois é! Níl mé imithe 30 seachtain fiú, ní bheadh seans ar bith aige.

english: Don't talk like that, Suzy.
irish: A Suzy, ná bí ag caint mar sin! B'fhéidir le cúnamh Dé nach dtarlóidh sé ar chor ar bith fós.

english: With the help of God, that mightn't even happen.
irish: A Suzy, ná bí ag caint mar sin! B'fhéidir le cúnamh Dé nach dtarlóidh sé ar chor ar bith fós.

english: With the help of God, it mightn't happen?
irish: B'fhéidir le cúnamh Dé! Go raibh míle maith agat a Ríona, is mór an faoiseamh é sin.

english: Thanks a bunch. What a consolation that is!
irish: A Suzy, beidh chuile shórt alright okay?

english: Everything will be alright, Suzy.
irish: A Suzy, beidh chuile shórt alright okay?

english: Either way, you'll be well looked after.
irish: Tabharfar aire mhaith duit anseo cibé céard a thárlós.

english: -Suzy Loughlin? -Declan Brosnan. Hello Ríona.
irish: -Suzy Loughlin? -Declan Brosnan.

english: Hi!
irish: -Hello Ríona. -Hi.

english: -I'd like a word with you. -Stay here Ríona.
irish: -Ba mhaith liom labhairt leat nóiméad -Fan Ríona.

english: Okay, if that's what you want.
irish: Ceart go leor más é sin atá uait. Phioc tú cara maith.

english: You have a great friend there!
irish: Tá muid an-bhródúil as Ríona. Duine de na banaltraí is fearr atá faoi oiliúint againn faoi láthair.

english: We're very proud of Ríona. She's one of the best trainee nurses.
irish: Tá muid an-bhródúil as Ríona. Duine de na banaltraí is fearr atá faoi oiliúint againn faoi láthair.

english: Okay, this is how it is.
irish: Seo é an chaoi a bhfuil sé..!

english: I spoke to the doctors in orthopaedics.
irish: Labhair mé leis na dochtúirí in orthopaedics agus tá gach seans go bhfuil cnámh do leicinn briste.

english: There's a strong possibility that your cheekbone is broken.
irish: Labhróidh Dochtúir eile leat faoi sin ar ball ach an rud is mó atá...

english: Another doctor will discuss that with you later.
irish: Labhróidh Dochtúir eile leat faoi sin ar ball ach an rud is mó atá...

english: My biggest concern is the bleeding from the womb.
irish: .ag déanamh imní domsa ná an fhuil a tháinig ón mbroinn.

english: What caused it?
irish: -Céard ba chúis leis? -Rud ar a dtugtar placenta praevia.

english: A condition called placenta praevia.
irish: Go bunúsach ciallaíonn sé go bhfuil laigeacht sa nasc idir an placenta agus an bhroinn.

english: It means that the connection between the placenta and the womb is weak.
irish: -Má éiríonn sé níos measa caithfimid Caesarean éigeandála a dhéanamh. -Caesarean?

english: If it worsens, we'll have to carry out an emergency Caesarean.
irish: -Má éiríonn sé níos measa caithfimid Caesarean éigeandála a dhéanamh. -Caesarean?

english: -Caesarean? -Don't worry.
irish: Ní gá a bheith buartha faoi.

english: We have a very experienced team.
irish: Tá foireann iontach againn agus ard taithí acu. Tabharfaidh siad an-aire duit.

english: I don't want a Caesarean.
irish: -Níl mé ag iarraidh Caesarean! -Ní tharlóidh sé muna mbíonn géarghá leis.

english: It'll only happen in the case of an emergency.
irish: Ní theastaíonn obráid uaim is cuma céard a tharlaíonn.

english: Whatever happens, I don't want an operation.
irish: Ní theastaíonn obráid uaim is cuma céard a tharlaíonn.

english: I'm sorry but if you start to bleed again, there'll be no alternative.
irish: Tá brón orm a Suzy ach má thosaíonn tú ag cur fola arís ní bheidh aon dul as. A Suzy tabharfaidh siad anaesthetic duit agus ní aireoidh tú tada.

english: They'll give you an anaesthetic. You won't feel a thing.
irish: A Suzy tabharfaidh siad anaesthetic duit agus ní aireoidh tú tada.

english: -General anaesthetic? -Yes.
irish: -General anaesthetic? -Sea. Sin é go díreach.

english: No, you have to keep the baby inside. I don't want an operation.
irish: No, caithfidh sibh an páiste a choinneáil istigh ansin. Ní theastaíonn aon obráid uaim.

english: That's what we're trying to avoid.
irish: Sin é atá muid ag iarraidh a dhéanamh ach caithfidh tú a thuiscint nach mbíonn aon rogha ann uaireanta.

english: However, you must understand that sometimes there's no alternative.
irish: Sin é atá muid ag iarraidh a dhéanamh ach caithfidh tú a thuiscint nach mbíonn aon rogha ann uaireanta.

english: -What have I done now? -That was HQ on the phone.
irish: Céard atá déanta anois agam? B'shin HQ ar an bhfón ansin. Tá siad ag cur cigire amach go Ros na Rún.

english: They're sending an inspector to Ros na Rún.
irish: O, chopáil siad on sa deireadh nach bhfuil aon chliú agat an ea?

english: They finally copped on that you're useless!
irish: O, chopáil siad on sa deireadh nach bhfuil aon chliú agat an ea?

english: They're investigating the previous sergeant actually.
irish: Tá siad ag fiosrú an sáirsint a bhí romham mar a tharlaíonn sé.

english: This is all very interesting but I'm on my way home.
irish: Breathnaigh tá sé seo uilig suimiúil ach tá mise ar an mbealach abhaile.

english: Look it! I don't want any trouble while the inspector is around.
irish: Féach níl mé ag iarraidh aon trioblóid nuair atá an cigire anseo, fan glan amach ó Daniel McLoughlin.

english: Stay well away from Daniel McLoughlin
irish: Féach níl mé ag iarraidh aon trioblóid nuair atá an cigire anseo, fan glan amach ó Daniel McLoughlin.

english: I had no intention of going near him.
irish: -Ní raibh aon notion agamsa dul gar don bhastard! -Go maith.

english: Good.
irish: -Ní raibh aon notion agamsa dul gar don bhastard! -Go maith.

english: Don't draw any attention to yourself and everything will be fine.
irish: Ná bí ag tarraingt aon airde ort féin ach an oiread agus beidh gach rud togha.

english: What has this got to do with me?
irish: Cén bhaint atá agamsa leis seo? Seo é mo chéad phost mar sháirsint agus tá mé anseo ar bhonn trialach.

english: This is my first job as sergeant and it's on a trial basis.
irish: Seo é mo chéad phost mar sháirsint agus tá mé anseo ar bhonn trialach.

english: If my brother goes around causing trouble, it'll go against me.
irish: Ní dhéanfaidh sé maitheas ar bith dom má mo dheartháir ag dul thart ag déanamh trioblóide san áit.

english: What are you doing here?
irish: Céard atá tusa ag déanamh anseo? Dúirt mé leat nár theastaigh uaim tú a fheiceáil.

english: I told you I didn't want to see you.
irish: Cén chaoi a bhféadfainn gan a thíocht agus tú istigh anseo? An bhfuil tú alright?

english: How could I not come to see you? Are you alright?
irish: Cén chaoi a bhféadfainn gan a thíocht agus tú istigh anseo? An bhfuil tú alright?

english: -Do I have to call the nurse? -What about the baby?
irish: -An nglaofaidh mé ar an mbanaltra? -Céard faoin bpáiste? Más é do thoil é a Suzy dhá nóiméad.

english: Please Suzy, just two minutes.
irish: Más é do thoil é a Suzy dhá nóiméad.

english: Look, I know you're fuming with me.
irish: Eist. Tá a fhios agam go bhfuil tú ar buile liom ach caithfidh tú a chreidiúnt nár chuimhnigh mise....

english: You must believe me when I say that I didn't know you'd be in danger.
irish: ..ar feadh soicind go mbeadh tú i mbaol.

english: I'm serious.
irish: I ndáiríre Suzy! Dá mbeadh a fhios agam go raibh contúirt dá laghad...

english: Had I known it'd be risky, I wouldn't have sent you.
irish: ..ann ní chuirfinn ann ar chor ar bith tú. Is cuma liom, is tusa is cúis leis ag deireadh an lae.

english: This is all your fault at the end of the day.
irish: Na habair é sin. Nach dtuigeann tú chomh mór agus a ghoilleann sé seo orm?

english: Don't say that! Can't you see how much this upsets me?
irish: Na habair é sin. Nach dtuigeann tú chomh mór agus a ghoilleann sé seo orm?

english: I went around to the brothel last night to kill that bastard.
irish: Chuaigh mé ag an teach striapachais sin aréir leis an mbastard sin a mharú.

english: There was no one there of course.
irish: Ar ndóigh ní raibh duine ná deoraí ann.

english: I guess he was afraid I'd go to the police.
irish: Is dócha go raibh faitíos air go rachainn ag na Gardaí.

english: Why didn't you say it was a brothel, when we were on the phone?
irish: Cén fáth nár dhúirt tú liom gur teach striapachais a bhí ann nuair a bhí tú ar an bhfón?

english: He told me it was an office.
irish: Dúirt sé sin liomsa gur oifig a bhí ann.

english: There was an office alright and plenty of bedrooms too!
irish: Bhí oifig aige ceart go leor. Agus neart seomraí leapan freisin.

english: God Almighty!
irish: Go sábhála Dia sin! Tá an-bhrón orm.

english: I'm so sorry.
irish: Go sábhála Dia sin! Tá an-bhrón orm.

english: I can't understand why it's bothering me so much.
irish: Níl a fhios agam beag ná beo cén fáth a bhfuil sé ag cur an oiread as dom a Pheadair.

english: It shouldn't bother you at all.
irish: Ní cheart go mbeadh sé ag cur as duitse beag ná mór mar a dhath riamh féin níor rinne tú as an mbealach.

english: You did nothing wrong.
irish: Ní cheart go mbeadh sé ag cur as duitse beag ná mór mar a dhath riamh féin níor rinne tú as an mbealach.

english: They just couldn't take Oscar from his family.
irish: Ní raibh ann mar a dearfá ach nach bhféadfaidís Oscairín a thógáil óna mhuintir féin.

english: I know that, Peadar.
irish: Tá a fhios agam é sin a Pheadair. Tá a fhios agam!

english: It'd be nice to mind him, just the two of us.
irish: Ach nach mbeadh sé go hálainn é a bheith againn dúinn féin le bheith ag tabhairt aire dó.

english: I'm so fond of him.
irish: -Tá an oiread ceana agam air. -Tá a fhios agam go bhfuil agus tá tú ag tabhairt aire dó chuile lá.

english: I know that, Máire, but you do take care of him every day.
irish: Agus an-aire! Ach ansin sé an rud nádúrach chomh maith...

english: And great care at that.
irish: Agus an-aire! Ach ansin sé an rud nádúrach chomh maith...

english: It's only natural to bring him to see his mother at the weekend.
irish: ..an dtuigeann tú go gcaithfidís é a thabhairt chomh fada lena mháthair ar an deireadh seachtaine.

english: There's nothing more you can do for him.
irish: Níos mó ní fhéadfadh tusa a dhéanamh dó a Mháire. Ní fhéadfá níos mó ná sin a dhéanamh dó nach iad féin a dúirt é.

english: Even they themselves said that.
irish: Agus ar a laghad tá codladh na hoíche againn ar an gcaoi seo.

english: At least this way we can get a night's sleep.
irish: Tuigim é sin go maith a Pheadair.

english: I know, Peadar.
irish: Tuigim é sin go maith a Pheadair.

english: It's just that I had my heart set on fostering Oscar this time around.
irish: Meastú gurb é go raibh sé leagtha amach agam i mo chroí istigh... ..a bhfuil a fhios agat gurb é Oscar a bheadh agam mar pháiste altramais an geámh seo?

english: That's not how it works, love.
irish: Ní shin é an chaoi a oibríonn rudaí a stór.

english: We should count ourselves lucky.
irish: Ach tá an t-ádh orainn. Tá Oscar agat chuile lá agus tá Evan sa teach béal dorais.

english: You mind Oscar every day and Evan is only next door.
irish: Ní é chuile shean bheirt a stór a bhfuil an méid sin den ádh orthu an chaoi a bhfuil an saol faoi láthair.

english: Not every old couple are that lucky in this day and age.
irish: Ní é chuile shean bheirt a stór a bhfuil an méid sin den ádh orthu an chaoi a bhfuil an saol faoi láthair.

english: You're right. We are lucky.
irish: Tá tú ceart a Pheadair. Tá an t-ádh orainn.

english: Máire.
I think you should have a chat with Berni about this.
irish: Sílim a Mháire gur cheart duit labhairt le Berni mar gheall air.

english: She thinks she's to blame that we didn't get Oscar.
irish: Tá an-mhilleán aici uirthi féin mar gheall nach bhfuair muid Oscar. Tá sí ag ceapadh gurb é féin ba chiontach.

english: I'll talk to her, don't worry.
irish: Labhróidh mise léi a Pheadair ná bíodh imní ar bith ort faoi sin.

english: I'm very proud of you.
irish: Tá mé an-bhródúil asat.

english: Why?
irish: -Muise tuige a Pheadair? -Tuige nach mbeadh?

english: Why wouldn't I be?
irish: Tá sé iontach tú féin agus Berni a fheiceáil mór lena chéile arís.

english: It's great to see yourself and Berni getting on again.
irish: Agus ní bhféadfadh sibh sampla níos fearr a thabhairt do Evan a Mháire...

english: You're setting Evan a great example.
irish: Agus ní bhféadfadh sibh sampla níos fearr a thabhairt do Evan a Mháire...

english: Seeing his mother and grandmother getting along.
irish: ..ná a mháthair agus a sheanmháthair a bheith mór lena chéile. Gabh i leith uait!

english: Let's go!
Are you going to report him to the Gardaí?
irish: An bhfuil tú ag dul á inseacht do na Gardaí? Is maith a d'fheilfeadh sé sin tú nach maith?

english: That would suit you, wouldn't it?
irish: Is maith a d'fheilfeadh sé sin tú nach maith?

english: That way, you wouldn't be suspected.
irish: -Ní bheadh aon amhras ann fútsa ansin. -Ach an stopfaidh tú don seafóid.

english: Stop that nonsense!
irish: Ní seafóid é!

english: It's not nonsense.
irish: Ní seafóid é!

english: You'll pay for what you did to me.
irish: Iocfaidh tú as an rud a rinne tú orm!

english: You're not using your head, Suzy.
irish: A Suzy, níl tú ag úsáid do chloiginn!

english: Both of us need to think of a way to get even with that bastard.
irish: Caithfidh muid smaoineamh ar bhealach le díoltas a bhaint as an mbastard sin...

english: But we need to go about it carefully.
irish: Ach caithfimid a bheith cúramach faoi.

english: Let bygones be bygones. We have to pull together, this is no joke.
irish: Come on a Suzy, caithfimid dearmad a dhéanamh ar chuile rud go dtí seo... ..agus tarraingt le chéile, ní joke ar bith é seo.

english: Who are you telling?
irish: Cé dó a bhfuil tú á inseacht?

english: Right!
I wouldn't recommend involving the Gardaí yet.
irish: Bhuel, ní mholfainn duit na Gardaí a tharraingt isteach sa scéal fós.

english: We're dealing with a dangerous man.
irish: -Fear contúirteach é seo. -Tuigim go rí-mhaith cén sórt fir é.

english: I know exactly what kind of a man he is.
Hello.
irish: Ar aghaidh ort a Shéamuis.

english: Are you busy?
irish: -Cruógach bail ó Dhia ort. -Ar ndóigh caithfidh duine éigin é a dhéanamh.

english: Someone's got to do it.
irish: Ar ndóigh dá mbeinn anseo go dtí lá speireadh na gcat ní dhéanfadh an dá leaid sin aon obair.

english: I could be here until kingdom come waiting for them to lift a finger!
irish: A dhiabhail níl siad chomh dona sin.

english: They can't be that bad.
irish: Ar ndóigh maidir le lucht óige na huaire seo, ní thuigeann siad céard é obair.

english: The youth of today don't know the meaning of work.
irish: Ar ndóigh maidir le lucht óige na huaire seo, ní thuigeann siad céard é obair.

english: Youth? Those two aren't in the prime of their youth anymore.
irish: Og? M'anam muise shílfeá go bhfuil na géaráin curtha go maith ag an mbeirt sin anois nach bhfuil siad sin óg... -..ar chor ar bith. -Cén mhaith an diabhal duit a bheith ag caint?

english: What are you talking about?
irish: -..ar chor ar bith. -Cén mhaith an diabhal duit a bheith ag caint?

english: I'm twice as old as the two of them.
irish: Nach bhfuil mise faoi dhó chomh sean le ceachtar acu agus tá trí oiread oibre déanta anseo ó mhaidin agam...

english: And I've done three times as much work as the two of them put together.
irish: ..agus atá déanta ag an mbeirt acu curtha le chéile.

english: They can go to hell for all I care!
irish: Ach téidis go mullach tigh an diabhail anois. Cuirfidís méar i mo ghiodán má thograíonn siad é.

english: The Lord save us and bless us!
irish: A dhiabhail! A dhiabhail goirim is coisricim thú!

english: I'm fine, Peadar.
irish: Tá mé ceart go leor a Pheadair.

english: Gosh!
irish: -A dhiabhail a Shéamuis! -An bhfuil tú alright a Shéamuis?

english: Are you alright, Séamus?
irish: Shílfeá gur rinne tú poillín in airde shílfeá sin.

english: You did a little somersault there!
irish: Shílfeá gur rinne tú poillín in airde shílfeá sin.

english: You should stop drinking at lunch time! You're not a young lad anymore.
irish: Ba cheart duit éirí as an ól sin ag am dinnéir! M'anam nach leaid óg tú níos mó. Seo.

english: Frig off you wasters!
irish: Ara frigeáililgí libh a dhá leadaí!

english: Is that your equipment?
irish: An leatsa an trealamh sin?

english: Is that your equipment?
irish: An leatsa an trealamh sin?

english: Yes, Sergeant.
irish: Sea a Sháirsint. Bhuel cuir in áit shábháilte é. Tá sé contúirteach fágtha mar sin i lár bhealaigh.

english: Put it in a safe place. It's causing a hazard there.
irish: Bhuel cuir in áit shábháilte é. Tá sé contúirteach fágtha mar sin i lár bhealaigh.

english: I'm sorry, Sergeant.
irish: Gabh mo leithscéal a Sháirsint, sé an chaoi a raibh muid ag obair ar an gcarr...

english: We're working on the car. Only for that, it wouldn't be there.
irish: ..agus ní raibh muid críochnaithe leis. Ní bheadh sé ann murach sin a Sháirsint.

english: It tripped Séamus.
irish: Tá sé tar éis tuisle a bhaint as Séamus.

english: Never mind, Séamus. Séamus is just fine.
irish: Ná bac le Séamus a Sháirsint. M'anam go bhfuil Séamus ceart go leor.

english: He wasn't hurt. There's no point making a big deal about it.
irish: Tá Séamus go breá. Níor gortaíodh ar chor ar bith é. Ní fiú a bheith ag tarraingt raice mar gheall air.

english: It'll be a big deal if it happens again.
irish: Beidh raic ann má tharlaíonn sé arís.

english: Now if you don't mind, put that equipment in a safe place.
irish: Anois, munar mhiste leat, cuir an trealamh in áit shábháilte.

english: And don't let it happen again.
irish: Agus ná lig dó tarlúint arís. Ceart go leor a Sháirsint.

english: Okay, Sergeant.
irish: Ceart go leor a Sháirsint.

english: Maybe I can't get my revenge on that bastard for what he did...
irish: B'fhéidir nach féidir liom díoltas a fháil ar an mbastard as an rud a rinne sé orm. Ach maidir leatsa..!

english: ..but as for you, that's quite another matter.
irish: Bhuel, sin scéal eile.

english: What good will that do you, Suzy?
irish: A Suzy, cén mhaith a dhéanfaidh sé sin duitse?

english: To see you down like I was.
irish: Tabharfaidh sé seans domsa tusa a fheiceáil thíos mar a bhí mise.

english: Or maybe I'll have a word with Fatman.
irish: Nó b'fhéidir go labhróidh mé le Fatman. Déarfaidh mé leis nach ngabhfaidh mé ag na Gardaí...

english: I could promise not to go to the Gardaí provided that he send out...
irish: ..má chuireann sé cúpla 'thug' amach do jab a dhéanamh ortsa...

english: ..a few thugs to do a job on you like the one you did on me.
irish: ..díreach mar a rinne tusa ormsa. An as do mheabhair atá tú?

english: Have you completely lost your mind?
irish: Fanfaidh tú glan amach uaidh sin nó maróidh sé an bheirt againn! Tá mé i ndáiríre a Suzy.

english: Stay away from him or he'll kill us both.
irish: Fanfaidh tú glan amach uaidh sin nó maróidh sé an bheirt againn! Tá mé i ndáiríre a Suzy.

english: I'm serious, Suzy.
I'll get even with him, sooner or later.
irish: Bainfidh mise díoltas as, luath nó mall. Ach caithfidh mé a bheith cúramach cén chaoi a dtabharfaidh mé faoi.

english: I just need to be careful how I go about it.
irish: Níl mé ag iarraidh do dhíoltas.

english: I don't want you to get even. I never want anything from you again.
irish: Níl mé ag iarraidh tada uait go deo arís.

english: Don't be like that, Suzy.
irish: A Suzy, ná bí mar sin.

english: Look at this as a way of bringing us close again.
irish: Breathnaigh ar seo mar bhealach le muid a thabhairt le chéile arís.

english: I want that. Don't you?
irish: Sin atá uaimse. Nach shin atá uaitse?

english: Look what he did to you!
irish: Jesus féach céard a rinne sé ort!

english: Get out of my sight.
irish: -Imigh leat as mo radharc. -Ach a Suzy!

english: Suzy!
irish: Okay, okay, tá mé ag imeacht.

english: Okay, okay, I'm leaving.
irish: Okay, okay, tá mé ag imeacht.

english: They were right at the end of the day.
irish: Ar ndóigh a Bherni, nach acu a bhí an ceart dáiríre.

english: I don't know how I thought you could pick a child yourself.
irish: Cén fáth ar cheap mé go bhféadfadh daoine dul thart ag piocadh gasúir dóibh féin ar an gcaoi sin?

english: I suppose that can't be done.
irish: Bhuel ní fhéadfadh, seans.

english: Any child they give you will be lucky.
irish: Ach cibé cén pháiste a thabharfaidh siad duit a Mháire bhuel beidh an t-ádh dearg orthu...

english: You're great with kids, everyone says it.
irish: -..mar tá tú iontach uilig le páistí. Deireann chuile dhuine é sin. -An-lá a mhná.

english: -Hello ladies. -Peadar!
irish: A Pheadair.

english: Any chance I could leave Evan with you this week?
irish: Breathnaigh, seans ar bith go bhféadfadh Evan tíocht anonn agat...

english: He said he'd love to go painting with you again.
irish: ..an tseachtain seo? Dúirt sé gur bhreá leis dul ag péinteáil leat arís

english: Of course, Berni. Evan is welcome any time he wants to call over.
irish: Gan dabht a Bherni! Tá fáilte roimh Evan am ar bith a dtogróidh sé féin é a stór.

english: Evainín!
irish: -Ara a Evainín! -Breathnaigh cé atá anseo!

english: Look who it is.
irish: Hello a Evan!

english: Hello Evan!
irish: -Cén chaoi a bhfuil tú? -Go maith.

english: -How are you? -Good.
irish: -An dtiocfaidh tú anuas ag Mamó amárach an dtiocfaidh? -Tiocfaidh.

english: Will you call over to Mamó tomorrow?
irish: -An dtiocfaidh tú anuas ag Mamó amárach an dtiocfaidh? -Tiocfaidh.

english: Yes.
irish: A muise maith an fear!

english: Good boy! Let's go inside and I'll show you the lovely paints I have.
irish: Fág seo isteach sa siopa in éindí liom anois go bhfeicfidh tú na péinteannaí deasa atá agam.

english: Bye, Evan.
irish: Anois! Slán a Evan!

english: Good boy!
irish: -Anois. -Maith an buachaill.

english: -There you go, Berni. -Thanks.
irish: -Anois a Bherni. -Go raibh maith agat.

english: Hold on a minute, Berni.
irish: -A Bherni, nóiméad amháin. -Céard é féin?

english: What?
irish: Bhuel éist soicind liom anois.

english: Hear me out.
irish: Tá rud éigin ag cur as d'Evan i gcónaí. Tá imní orm faoi.

english: Something is still bothering Evan. I'm worried about him.
irish: Agus tá a fhios againn uilig go bhfuil sé an-éasca imní a chur ortsa.

english: We all know how easy it is to worry you!
irish: No, breathnaigh. An bhfuil a fhios agat, nuair a chuaigh mé síos...

english: When I picked him up at the naíonra, he was very quiet.
irish: ..ag an naíonra dá phiocadh suas bhí sé an-chiúin.

english: He didn't even want to say goodbye to the other kids.
irish: Ní raibh sé sásta slán a fhágáil ag na gasúir eile fiú. Tá mé ag rá leat a Bherni, tá rud éigin mícheart leis.

english: There's something bothering him, believe me.
irish: Níl tada mícheart le mo mhac-sa!

english: There's nothing wrong with my son.
irish: Níl tada mícheart le mo mhac-sa!

english: Look Berni.
irish: Breathnaigh a Bherni, níl mé ag iarraidh a bheith...

english: I don't mean to upset you. I'm just worried about Evan.
irish: ..ag cur isteach ortsa níl mé sin. Imní atá orm faoi Evan.

english: It's probably just his wretch of a mother...
irish: Céard a bheadh mícheart leis, meas tú? Ach go bhfuil an chailleach sin de mháthair atá aige dá bhualadh...

english: ..who's beating him black and blue!
irish: -..agus dá fháisceadh ab ea? -Ní shin é a dúirt mé ar chor ar bith

english: That's not what I'm saying.
irish: -..agus dá fháisceadh ab ea? -Ní shin é a dúirt mé ar chor ar bith

english: Yeah right. I'll tell you what the best thing for Evan is.
irish: O ní shea muis! Bhuel inseoidh mise duit anois céard é an rud is fearr go bhféadfaidh tusa a dhéanamh... ..d'Evan ag an bpointe seo. Fan glan amach uaidh agus tabhair aire do do ghraithe féin!

english: For you to stay away from him and mind your own business!
irish: ..d'Evan ag an bpointe seo. Fan glan amach uaidh agus tabhair aire do do ghraithe féin!

english: You have to tell the Gardaí.
irish: Caithfidh tú é seo a inseacht do na Gardaí a Suzy. Ní fhéadfaidh tú ligean cead dó fáil away leis seo.

english: You can't let him get away with this.
irish: I mean breathnaigh ort féin!

english: I mean, look at yourself.
irish: -Sé Daniel a rinne é nach é? -Níl mé ag iarraidh labhairt faoi.

english: Is was Daniel who did it, wasn't it?
irish: -Sé Daniel a rinne é nach é? -Níl mé ag iarraidh labhairt faoi.

english: I don't want to talk about it.
You have to do something about it.
irish: Caithfidh tú rud éigin a dhéanamh faoi. For God's sake a Suzy, bhuail sé tú!

english: For God's sake Suzy, he beat you.
irish: Leag as a Ríona, más é do thoil é!

english: Knock it off, Ríona. Please.
irish: Tá sé sách dona mar atá gan a bheith ag dul ag na Gardaí. Tá mo dhóthain fadhbanna agam cheana féin.

english: Things are bad enough already without going to the Gardaí.
irish: Tá sé sách dona mar atá gan a bheith ag dul ag na Gardaí. Tá mo dhóthain fadhbanna agam cheana féin.

english: I have enough problems as it is.
I'll be back in a while. Okay?
irish: Beidh mé ar ais ar ball okay?

english: Hello!
irish: Hello?

english: Thanks very much, Róise.
irish: Go raibh míle maith agat a Róise.

english: Do you have the scripts for those new ads, Róise?
irish: An bhfuil na scripteanna agat do na fógraí nua sin a Róise?

english: Róise.
irish: -A Róise? -D'fhág mé ar do dheasc iad.

english: I left them on your desk.
irish: -A Róise? -D'fhág mé ar do dheasc iad.

english: Thanks.
irish: Go raibh maith agat.
Yeah?

english: Labhrás! What can I do for you?
irish: A Labhráis! Céard is féidir liom a dhéanamh duit?

english: I've got them here.
irish: Tá siad anseo agam.

english: Thank you.
irish: Go raibh maith agat.

english: Is that the last of the information?
irish: An bhfuil an t-eolas ar fad istigh anseo anois?

english: Yes.
irish: -Tá. -Go maith, go raibh maith agat.

english: Good. Thank you.
irish: Féach, tá áthas orm an rud seo ar fad a bheith curtha taobh thiar dínn.

english: Look, I'm delighted that we've put all this behind us.
irish: Féach, tá áthas orm an rud seo ar fad a bheith curtha taobh thiar dínn.

english: I never thought you'd allow censorship like this to happen.
irish: Níor cheap mé go ligfeá do chinsireacht mar seo a tharlú a Labhráis.

english: It was a board decision. There's nothing I can do about it.
irish: Cinneadh boird a bhí ann a Rory. Ní féidir liomsa tada a dhéanamh faoi. Ní cinneadh boird a bhí ann, cinneadh pearsanta a bhí ann...

english: It was a personal decision by the chairman and well you know it.
irish: ..ó chathaoirleach an bhoird agus tá a fhios agatsa go maith é.

english: The decision has been made and you should accept it.
irish: Tá an cinneadh déanta anois a Rory agus ba chóir duit glacadh leis.

english: I'm not willing to accept it.
irish: Yeah bhuel níl mé sásta glacadh leis! Táim ag iarraidh go bpléifidh an bord stiúrtha é.

english: I want the board of directors to discuss it.
irish: O!

english: But Jason, what am I supposed to tell her?
irish: Ach a Jason, céard atá mise ceapaithe a inseacht di?

english: Can't you just come and visit?
irish: Níl ann ach go bhféadfadh tú just teacht ar cuairt aici, please!

english: Please.
irish: Níl ann ach go bhféadfadh tú just teacht ar cuairt aici, please!

english: She has no one else.
irish: Ach níl aon duine eile aici.

english: You can't..! Jason?
irish: A Jason ní fhéadfaidh tú..! A Jason?

english: Were you in to see her?
irish: An raibh tú istigh ag amharc uirthi?

english: Yeah.
irish: -Yeah bhí. -Agus caidé mar atá sí?

english: How is she?
irish: -Yeah bhí. -Agus caidé mar atá sí?

english: Okay, I suppose.
irish: Maith go leor is dócha.

english: Will the baby be okay?
irish: Ach beidh an leanbh ceart go leor an mbeidh?

english: We don't know.
irish: Níl a fhios againn.

english: The poor girl.
irish: An bhean bhocht. Ar labhair sí leis na Gardaí faoi dtaobh de go fóill?

english: Has she spoken to the Gardaí yet?
irish: Ar labhair sí leis na Gardaí faoi dtaobh de go fóill?

english: No, she hasn't.
irish: -No níor labhair. -Cad tuige?

english: Why not?
irish: Mar níl sí ag iarraidh tada a rá leo.

english: Because she doesn't want to tell them anything.
irish: Mar níl sí ag iarraidh tada a rá leo.

english: She's probably right.
irish: Is dócha go bhfuil an ceart aici mar sin.

english: No, she isn't.
irish: Níl an ceart aici!

english: Whoever did that to her should pay for it.
irish: Cibé cé a rinne é sin uirthi, ba cheart go n-íocfaidís as.

english: That's why I'm thinking about going to the Gardaí.
irish: Sin é an fáth go bhfuil barúil agam féin dul ag na Gardaí. -Tusa? -Yeah.

english: You?
irish: -Tusa? -Yeah.

english: Yeah, there's no alternative.
irish: Bhuel níl aon rogha eile ann an bhfuil?

english: I wouldn't recommend that.
irish: Níor mholfainnse duit sin a dhéanamh.

english: Why not?
irish: Cén fáth? Bhuel níl a fhios againn caidé a tharla. Ní fhaca muid a dhath.

english: We don't know what happened.
irish: Bhuel níl a fhios againn caidé a tharla. Ní fhaca muid a dhath.

english: We didn't see anything.
I've seen enough. Haven't you?
irish: Bhuel chonaic mise mo dhóthain, nach bhfaca tusa?

english: I don't know about that.
irish: Níl a fhios agam.

english: Don't tell me you don't suspect him.
irish: Come on a John Joe! Ná habair liom nach bhfuil tusa féin amhrasach faoi?

english: Who?
irish: Faoi cé?

english: Daniel, who else?
irish: Daniel, cé eile?

english: Come on!
irish: Come on, an síleann tusa i ndáiríre go mbualfadh Daniel a iníon féin?

english: Do you really think Daniel would beat his own daughter?
irish: Cé eile a dhéanfadh é?

english: Who else would do it?
irish: Agus inis dom, cén fáth go mbeadh sí ag iarraidh é a chosaint?

english: And why else would she protect him?
irish: Agus inis dom, cén fáth go mbeadh sí ag iarraidh é a chosaint?

english: You're not one hundred per cent sure about that.
irish: Níl a fhios agatsa é sin anois go hiomlán cruinn. Go dtí go ndéanann sise gearán oifigiúil,...

english: Until she makes an official complaint...
irish: ..ní cheart duit a bheith ag fágáil an locht ar aon duine.

english: ..you shouldn't go accusing anyone.
irish: B'fhéidir go bhfuil cúis mhaith aici sin a dhéanamh.

english: -She could have her reasons. -What do you mean?
irish: Cén chaoi?

english: After all that happened with Jason...
irish: Bhuel i ndiaidh an rud seo uilig a tharla le Jason tá seans nach gcreidfeadh aon duine í.

english: ..there's a chance that no one will believe her.
irish: O ag caint ar Jason, an bhfuil sé ag teacht ag amharc uirthi?

english: Speaking of which, is Jason coming to see her?
irish: O ag caint ar Jason, an bhfuil sé ag teacht ag amharc uirthi?

english: I only just told him she's in hospital.
irish: Níl mé ach tar éis inseacht dó go bhfuil sí san ospidéal.

english: And?
irish: Agus?

english: And he's not interested.
irish: Agus níl aon suim aige ann.

english: A half a ton. I'll order that now and I'll ring you when it arrives.
irish: Leath thonna. Ordóidh mise é sin anois agus cuirfidh mé glaoch ort nuair a bheidh sé tagtha. -Togha a Mhichíl. -Alright a Chóilín. Lá maith agat a dheartháir.

english: -Alright Micheál. -Goodbye, Cóilín.
irish: -Togha a Mhichíl. -Alright a Chóilín. Lá maith agat a dheartháir.

english: Come here a second.
irish: -Soicind amháin. -O a Chiaráin, céard a bhí le rá ag an gcuntasóir?

english: What did the accountant have to say?
irish: -Soicind amháin. -O a Chiaráin, céard a bhí le rá ag an gcuntasóir?

english: What we expected.
irish: Bhuel díreach mar a cheap muid. Níor shroich muid na figiúirí a bhí beartaithe againn.

english: We didn't reach our targets.
irish: Mura ndearnamaid rud éigin go beo, tá mé ag ceapadh go mbeidh fadhb againn.

english: If we don't make changes very soon we're going to have problems.
irish: Agus céard is féidir linn a dhéanamh?

english: What can we do?
irish: Agus céard is féidir linn a dhéanamh?

english: A new advertising campaign.
irish: Feachtas nua poiblíochta. Bileogaí, b'fhéidir fógraí raidió.

english: Fliers, maybe a radio ad.
irish: A no, níl aon mhaith ansin. Ní bheadh aon mhaith ann. Caithfimid cuimhniú ar sheift éicint nua, caithfimid sin.

english: That won't work.
irish: A no, níl aon mhaith ansin. Ní bheadh aon mhaith ann. Caithfimid cuimhniú ar sheift éicint nua, caithfimid sin.

english: We have to offer something new, something no one else is providing.
irish: Rud éigin nach bhfuil ag aon duine eile, an bhfuil a fhios agat?

english: Any ideas?
irish: -Aon smaointí agat féin? -Níl ach caithfimid cuimhniú ar rud éigin go beo.

english: No but we have to think of something quick.
irish: Mar níl leath ár ndóthain graithe ag dul amach as an áit seo.

english: We don't do enough business.
irish: Mar níl leath ár ndóthain graithe ag dul amach as an áit seo.

english: Nor is there enough work being done around here either.
irish: Níl a mh'anam ná leath a ndóthain oibre á dhéanamh ach an oiread ann.

english: They carry out these kinds of operations every day.
irish: Déanann siad obráidí mar seo chuile lá. Agus deineann daoine botúin. Cailltear daoine.

english: Mistakes are made and people die.
irish: Agus deineann daoine botúin. Cailltear daoine.

english: Come on Suzy. Nothing is going to happen to you.
irish: Come on a Suzy, níl tada ag dul ag tarlú duit.

english: What does it matter? I wouldn't be much of a loss anyway.
irish: Is cuma ar aon chaoi? Ba bheag an chailliúint mé.

english: Suzy, don't say things like that.
irish: -A Suzy! Ná bí ag rá rudaí mar sin! -Cén fáth?

english: Why not? It would solve everyone's problems if I died.
irish: Nach maith a d'fheilfeadh sé chuile dhuine dá bhfaighinn bás? Bheadh fadhbanna mo Dhaid réitithe agus fadbhanna Janet.

english: My Dad's problems would be solved as would Janet's.
irish: Agus bheadh 'clean slate' ag Jason don chuid eile dá shaol.

english: And Jason would have a clean slate for the rest of his life.
irish: -Sin seafóid agus is maith atá a fhios agat é. -Meas tú?

english: That's rubbish and well you know it.
irish: -Sin seafóid agus is maith atá a fhios agat é. -Meas tú?

english: So where's Jason? Why isn't he here?
irish: Cá bhfuil Jason anois mar sin? Cén fáth nach bhfuil sé anseo?

english: I don't know.
irish: Níl a fhios agam. Seans nach bhfuil a fhios aige tada faoi seo fós.

english: He probably doesn't know about this yet.
It serves me right.
irish: -Tá sé sách maith agam nach bhfuil? -Níl. Níl sé seo tuillte ag aon duine

english: No! No one deserves this.
irish: -Tá sé sách maith agam nach bhfuil? -Níl. Níl sé seo tuillte ag aon duine

english: Do you know the real reason why I didn't go ahead with the abortion?
irish: An bhfuil a fhios agatsa i ndáiríre cén fáth nach ndeachaigh mé ar aghaidh leis an nginmhilleadh?

english: Yes. Because you couldn't kill that little baby.
irish: Tá a fhios. Mar ní raibh tú in ann an páistín beag sin a mharú.

english: I wish that were true.
irish: Faraor nach fíor duit é.

english: I was afraid.
irish: Faitíos.

english: Making that decision took a lot of courage, Suzy.
irish: A Suzy, thóg an cinneadh sin go leor leor misnigh.

english: Not courage but cowardice.
irish: Ní misneach ach cladhaireacht a bhí ann.

english: I had an abortion before.
irish: Bhí ginmhilleadh agam cheana.

english: I was well into my pregnancy when it was carried out.
irish: Bhí mé réasúnta fada chun cinn nuair a rinne siad é agus bhí fadhbanna ann.

english: There were complications.
I backed out of the abortion because I couldn't go through...
irish: Dhiúltaigh mé don ghinmhilleadh mar nach bhféadfainn dul tríd an obráid.

english: ..with the operation.
irish: Agus anois tá siad ag iarraidh mé a chur faoi scian chun an páiste a shábháil.

english: Now they want me to go under the knife to save the baby.
irish: Agus anois tá siad ag iarraidh mé a chur faoi scian chun an páiste a shábháil.

english: What would you do to entice customers who had left you to come back?
irish: Céard a dhéanfadh tusa le custaiméirí a d'imigh uait a mhealladh ar ais arís?

english: The first thing I'd do is review the company's assets.
irish: Bhuel sé an chéad rud a dhéanfainn ach breathnú ar na hacmhainní atá ag an gcomhlacht.

english: What are your assets?
irish: Céard iad na hacmhainní atá agaibh?

english: We're conveniently close to customers.
irish: Ar ndóigh, tá muid gar dóibh. Tá aithne ag daoine orainn, aithne againn orthu. Céard eile?

english: We know people.
irish: Ar ndóigh, tá muid gar dóibh. Tá aithne ag daoine orainn, aithne againn orthu. Céard eile?

english: What else?
You have another asset that neither of you thought of.
irish: Tá acmhainn amháin eile anois agaibh nár chuimhnigh ceachtar agaibh air. Agus acmhainn nach bhfuil sibh ag baint a leath a ndóthain úsáid as.

english: An asset you're not using half enough.
irish: -Agus céard é sin? -Séamus Mhicil Tom.

english: -What? -Séamus Mhicil Tom.
irish: -Agus céard é sin? -Séamus Mhicil Tom.

english: Séamus!
irish: -Séamus? -M'anam nach údar gáire ar bith é a dheartháir.

english: It's no laughing matter.
irish: Tá aithne ag Séamus ar dhaoine thoir agus thiar. Bíonn sé imeacht chuig na haontaí, ag na margaidh.

english: Séamus knows everyone for miles around.
irish: Tá aithne ag Séamus ar dhaoine thoir agus thiar. Bíonn sé imeacht chuig na haontaí, ag na margaidh.

english: He goes to the fairs.
irish: Tá sé in ann an bhleid a bhualadh ar dhaoine.

english: He knows how to get around people.
irish: Tá sé in ann an bhleid a bhualadh ar dhaoine.

english: Séamus knows how things work.
irish: Tuigeann Séamus a dheartháir cén chaoi a n-oibríonn rudaí...

english: An asset like that can't be bought.
irish: ..agus m'anam nach féidir deathoil mar sin a cheannach.

english: Gosh!
irish: ..agus m'anam nach féidir deathoil mar sin a cheannach.

english: You could send Séamus out to local farmers to drum up business.
irish: Dá gcuirfeadh sibhse anois a Mhichael Séamus amach... ..go dtí na feirmeoirí áitiúla mar a déarfá ag cuartú ordaithe..!

english: Give them a high-quality service and they'll stay with you.
irish: Bhuel bheadh sé suas daoibhse ansin an bhfuil a fhios agat,... ..seirbhís de ardchaighdeán ar fad a thabhairt dóibh. D'fhanfadh siad libh as sin amach.

english: You're right, Peadar.
irish: Tá an ceart agat a Pheadair.

english: You could go further afield for orders.
irish: Agus a Mhichael, d'fhéadfá tusa ansin dul níos faide ó bhaile nuair a bhíonn tú ag cuartú ordaithe.

english: Ciarán could easily manage the store.
irish: Agus bheadh Ciarán in ann bainistiú a dhéanamh ar an stór go héasca.

english: Fair play to you Peadar. I'd never have thought of that.
irish: Fair play duit a Pheadair ní chuimhneoinn go brách air a dheartháir, ní chuimhneoinn sin.

english: Why didn't we think of that?
irish: Cén fáth nár smaoinigh muide air sin?

english: There's only one problem.
irish: -Níl ach aon fhaidhbín bheag amháin anois leis an bplean seo. -Céard í sin?

english: What?
irish: -Níl ach aon fhaidhbín bheag amháin anois leis an bplean seo. -Céard í sin?

english: Séamus Mhicil Tom! He's not a happy camper these days.
irish: Séamus Mhicil Tom. Níl sé an-sásta na laethanta seo. Caithfimid é a mhealladh ar bord ar chaoi éigin.

english: We'll have to entice him somehow.
irish: Caithfimid é a mhealladh ar bord ar chaoi éigin.

english: That'll be your biggest obstacle.
irish: M'anam gurb shin í an fhadhb is mó a bheas agaibh mar nuair a bhuailfidh an cantal Séamus, a dheartháir,...

english: When Séamus gets into a mood, he's as stubborn as a mule.
irish: ..an collach muice féin níl chomh stobarnáilte leis.

english: Bye, Róise.
irish: Slán a Róise.

english: Never mind her.
irish: Ná bac léi-siúd.

english: Caitríona.
irish: -A Chaitríona? -Sea.

english: Yeah?
irish: -A Chaitríona? -Sea.

english: Have you told Vince yet about me?
irish: Ar dúirt tú tada le Vince fós fúmsa?

english: I haven't said a word.
irish: Níor dúirt mé tada. Ná bíodh imní ar bith ort, ní dhéarfaidh mise focal leis.

english: Don't worry, I won't mention a thing.
irish: Ná bíodh imní ar bith ort, ní dhéarfaidh mise focal leis.

english: Thanks.
irish: Go raibh maith agat.

english: I'm not doing it for your sake.
irish: Ní ar mhaithe leatsa atá mé á dhéanamh.

english: It's better for Vince not to know.
irish: -O -Tá sé níos fearr do Vince muna bhfuil a fhios aige.

english: I thought he'd get back with you if you told him.
irish: Ach shíl mé go dtiocfadh sé ar ais agat dá n-inseodh tú dó é?

english: That's what he said.
irish: Sin é a dúirt sé. Ach tá aithne níos fearr agamsa ar Vince.

english: But I know Vince much better than that.
irish: Cuma cé a deir sé, ní mhaithfeadh sé domsa é.

english: Regardless of what he says, he'd never forgive me or you either.
irish: Ná duitse ach oiread.

english: If you don't tell him, you'll definitely lose him.
irish: Ach mura ndeireann tú tada leis, beidh sé caillte agat ar aon nós.

english: Maybe this sounds strange coming from me...
irish: Ach, b'fhéidir go bhfuil sé seo aisteach ag teach uaimse ach..! Nach rachfá sa tseans?

english: ..but won't you even take the chance?
irish: Nach rachfá sa tseans?

english: You want to get back with him, right?
irish: Tá tú ag iarraidh a bheith ar ais leis nach bhfuil?

english: Rory, if Vince found out about me and you, he'd lose the head.
irish: A Rory, dá bhfaigheadh Vince amach fútsa, agus fúmsa,... ..chaillfeadh sé an bloc!

english: You'd end up in Casualty.
irish: Bheadh tusa istigh i gcasualty, seans go mbeadh Vince sa bpríosún.

english: Vince would probably end up in prison
irish: Cén mhaith a dhéanfadh sé sin d'aon duine?

english: What use would that be to anyone?
irish: Cén mhaith a dhéanfadh sé sin d'aon duine?

english: Your secret is safe with me, I won't say a word.
irish: A Rory, tá tú sábháilte liomsa. Ní dhéarfaidh mise focal leis.

english: Róise on the other hand is another matter entirely.
irish: Ach Róise..! Sin scéal eile ar fad.

english: I don't think they'll go to the Gardaí. For another while anyway.
irish: Ní shílim go rachaidh siad chuig na Gardaí, go fóill ar scor ar bith. -Cén fáth a gceapann tú é sin? -Bhuel bhí mé ag caint le Ríona...

english: Why do you say that?
irish: -Cén fáth a gceapann tú é sin? -Bhuel bhí mé ag caint le Ríona...

english: I was talking to Ríona earlier.
irish: ..ar ball agus níl sise ag dul ag déanamh a dhath ar bith.

english: She's not doing anything about it. She's leaving it up to Suzy.
irish: -Tá sí á fhágáil ag Suzy. -Is mór an faoiseamh é sin.

english: That's a relief.
irish: An bhfuil sí fós ag rá go bhfuil sí ag rámhaille go bhfuil sí faoin é a chur i mo leith-se?

english: Is she still threatening to blame me for it?
irish: An bhfuil sí fós ag rá go bhfuil sí ag rámhaille go bhfuil sí faoin é a chur i mo leith-se?

english: Suzy?
irish: -Suzy? -No, Banríon Shasana.

english: No! The Queen of England!
irish: -Suzy? -No, Banríon Shasana.

english: Not as far as I know.
irish: Níl, de réir mar a thuigim.

english: I thought you spoke to her.
irish: Ach shíl mé gur labhair tú léi? D'amharc mé isteach ach bhí Ríona ansin fosta.

english: I looked in on her but Ríona was there too.
irish: D'amharc mé isteach ach bhí Ríona ansin fosta.

english: Imagine my own daughter accusing me of lies like that!
irish: Samhlaigh m'iníon féin ag cur bréaga mar sin i mo leith?

english: She hasn't done it so far.
irish: Bhuel ní dhearna sí go fóill é agus fiú amháin, má dhéanann sure beidh tú ceart go leor.

english: Even if she does, you'll be fine.
irish: Bhuel ní dhearna sí go fóill é agus fiú amháin, má dhéanann sure beidh tú ceart go leor.

english: Provided you didn't do it.
irish: An fhad agus nach dearna tú é.

english: Do you think I did it?
irish: An gceapann tusa gur mise a rinne é?

english: Fatman did it. Right?
irish: An Fatman a rinne é, nárbh é?

english: Yes. I'll kill that bastard when I get a hold of him.
irish: Sé! -Fan go mbéarfaidh mise ar an mbastard sin, maróidh mé é. -An Fatman?

english: Fatman?
irish: -Fan go mbéarfaidh mise ar an mbastard sin, maróidh mé é. -An Fatman?

english: I've been trying to ring him but he's not answering.
irish: Tá mé ag cur glaoch air ó shin ach níl sé ag freagairt an fóin.

english: He probably doesn't want to talk to you until you cool down.
irish: Is dócha nach bhfuil sé ag iarraidh labhairt leat go dtí go socróidh tú síos.

english: If he thinks I'll forgive him he has another thing coming.
irish: Má cheapann sé go maithfidh mise dó é tá dul amú air. Ghortaigh sé Suzy. Chuir sé saol a páiste i mbaol.

english: He hurt Suzy and put the baby's life in danger.
irish: Iocfaidh sé go daor as, cuma cé hé féin.

english: I don't care who he is, he'll pay for it.
irish: Déan do chomhairle féin, ach ní tharraingeoinnse an Fatman ar mo thóir.

english: Do as you like but I for one wouldn't draw that Fatman on me.
irish: D'fhéadfadh sé tú féin agus Suzy a fhágáil faoi na fóid. Droch-airteagal atá ann.

english: He could end up killing you and Suzy. He's a bad article.
irish: D'fhéadfadh sé tú féin agus Suzy a fhágáil faoi na fóid. Droch-airteagal atá ann.

english: I know. I'm not that stupid.
irish: Tá a fhios agam, níl mé chomh hamaideach sin.

english: I'll catch up with him some day when he least expects it.
irish: Ach tiocfaidh mise suas leis. Lá éigin nuair nach mbeidh sé ag súil leis agus bainfidh mé díoltas as.

english: And I'll get revenge.
irish: Lá éigin nuair nach mbeidh sé ag súil leis agus bainfidh mé díoltas as.

english: Good luck with that!
irish: Adh mór leat a mhac.

english: Vince hi, come in!
irish: A Vince, hi, tar isteach!

english: I came to collect my stuff.
irish: Táim ag iarraidh mo chuid stuif a fháil.

english: Do you want a cup of tea or a glass of wine maybe?
irish: Ar mhaith leat cupán tae? Nó b'fhéidir gloine fíona?

english: No, thanks.
irish: -Ní bheidh, go raibh maith agat. -A Vince, nach bhféadfaimid labhairt faoi seo fiú?

english: Can't we even talk about this?
irish: Céard is fiú labhairt faoi, nuair nach bhfuil tusa sásta inseacht dom faoi céard a tharla.

english: What's the point when you won't even tell me what happened?
irish: -Tá a fhios agat céard a tharla? -No níl a fhios.

english: -You know what happened. -No I don't.
irish: -Tá a fhios agat céard a tharla? -No níl a fhios.

english: Now's your chance. Who were you with?
irish: Ach seo é do sheans. Cé leis a bhí tú?

english: Out with it! Who is he?
irish: Abair amach é! Cé hé féin?

english: It's your choice. I'm leaving.
irish: Sé do rogha féin é. Tá mise ag imeacht.

english: Please Vince!
irish: A Vince le do thoil! A Vince!

english: Vince!
irish: A Vince!
Oh no!
Fotheidil: Europus
